messages.content: Add an FAQ section with 4 common questions people ask before starting a vending machine business.

context.title: Coffee vending for offices
context.metaDescription: Coffee vending machines for modern workplaces.

props.heading: Old headline
props.body: Coffee vending machines for offices keep teams stocked with fresh drinks without anyone owning a brewing rota. A managed machine covers restocking, cleaning, and fault response so facilities can stay focused on the building.

faqItems.question: What utilities are needed to support a coffee vending machine?
faqItems.answer: Most modern machines require a standard dedicated power outlet and a connection to a potable water line. If a direct water connection is unavailable, some models offer self-contained water tanks that can be manually refilled by your team or the service provider. You should confirm your space layout, electrical access, and water proximity before finalizing the equipment model.

faqItems.question: How does the restocking process work for my office?
faqItems.answer: The frequency of restocking typically depends on your team's consumption volume and the machine's canister capacity. A managed service agreement often includes scheduled site visits to replenish beans, milk, sugar, and cups. If you prefer to manage inventory in-house, your provider should offer a clear ordering process and training on how to perform daily maintenance.

faqItems.question: Who is responsible for machine maintenance and cleaning?
faqItems.answer: Maintenance generally falls into two categories: daily hygiene and technical servicing. Daily tasks, such as wiping down surfaces or emptying waste bins, are often handled by office staff, whereas complex technical repairs or descaling procedures are the responsibility of the service provider. Verify the response time for technical call-outs in your service agreement to ensure minimal downtime for your staff.

faqItems.question: What are the common payment and service models?
faqItems.answer: There are several cost models available, ranging from full-service rental agreements to purchasing the equipment outright. Rental plans often bundle the machine, maintenance, and product supply into a single periodic fee, which can help keep monthly costs predictable. Alternatively, buying the unit gives you more control but shifts the responsibility of repairs and supply procurement entirely to your organization.

input.body: Addressing common questions about the requirements, planning, and operational considerations when getting started with commercial coffee solutions for your workplace.
input.title: Questions about setting up office coffee solutions
